title: Remotes, Refspecs, Pruning, and Safe Publication · Repository Checkpoint
summary: A maintainer review compares near-miss operations for remotes, refspecs, pruning, and safe publication and requires the smallest safe distributed ref mapping and publication safety plan with topology and content evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for distributed ref mapping and publication safety plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Parse positive and negative fetch and push refspecs, wildcard mappings, force markers, deletion, matching, and configured defaults.
Reason about advertised refs, negotiation, shallow or filtered boundaries, tag following, prune, prune-tags, and remote-tracking updates.
Predict fast-forward acceptance by namespace and object type and distinguish local preflight from server receive policy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Gita Refspec Source Destination · changed case
steps.instruction:
quiz case — Case G27 is a quiz in a disposable repository. publication is safe only if remote main still equals the reviewed b00b1e5 lease value. The learner must predict the explicit source, destination, and expected old ID expose overwrite authority before mutation, run git push --dry-run --force-with-lease=refs/heads/main:b00b1e5 origin HEAD:refs/heads/main, explain the dry run accepts the exact mapping in simulation and records that no network changed, test this changed condition: advance remote main to c0ffee0 and require the explicit lease to reject publication, and preserve this recovery boundary: retain the old and proposed remote IDs plus a local recovery ref before authorized push.

Which action demonstrates Gita Refspec Source Destination without dropping earlier contracts?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for parse positive and negative fetch and push refspecs, wildcard mappings, force markers, deletion, matching, and configured defaults.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the Gita Refspec Source Destination evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write the quiz evidence note for Gita Multi Remote Publication: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Case G34 is a quiz in a disposable repository. publication is safe only if remote main still equals the reviewed b00b1e5 lease value. The learner must predict the explicit source, destination, and expected old ID expose overwrite authority before mutation, run git push --dry-run --force-with-lease=refs/heads/main:b00b1e5 origin HEAD:refs/heads/main, explain the dry run accepts the exact mapping in simulation and records that no network changed, test this changed condition: advance remote main to c0ffee0 and require the explicit lease to reject publication, and preserve this recovery boundary: retain the old and proposed remote IDs plus a local recovery ref before authorized push.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Capture remote URLs, authentication boundary, exact source and destination, expected old and new IDs, server result, and post-fetch verification.
